title: salix japonica subf. fauriei
intro: Salix japonica subf. fauriei, also known as Japanese willow or Fauriei willow, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Salicaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high salicylic acid content, similar to that of white willow. The health benefits of Salix japonica subf. fauriei include reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as its potential to help manage symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional Asian medicine.
